page: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.6 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. No local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between processing power and memory performance. The AMD EPYC processor demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average range. Memory subsystem benchmarks show significant limitations, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the bottom 10% and uncached memory bandwidth read/write speeds categorized as weak. Similarly, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation tasks underperform, landing in the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the c6a.xlarge offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance, though the low memory bandwidth and 8.0 GB RAM capacity present clear bottlenecks for memory-intensive applications. This instance is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, and lightweight containerized applications, but is less optimal for large-scale database operations or memory-heavy data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks. However, its memory subsystem presents a notable tradeoff, with memory bandwidth and PassMark memory scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% ranges. Lacking local storage and GPU acceleration, the instance relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads such as single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, and basic containerized microservices, but is constrained for memory-intensive database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits weak memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing power with a lean memory footprint for compute-focused workloads.

4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for single-threaded tasks and lightweight web serving
